  "text": "The Kubernetes Ingress API migration from v1beta1 to v1 (and from extensions/v1beta1) involves multiple layers of conversion, validation, and storage configuration. Here are the key findings:\n\n## Storage Version and Version Priority\nThe storage version is **v1** (networking.k8s.io/v1), as established in pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go:41 via SetVersionPriority(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion). The storage version hash for ingresses/v1 is \"39NQlfNR+bo=\" (pkg/controlplane/storageversionhashdata/data.go:63).\n\n## Conversion Functions\nConversion between versions is handled through both manual and auto-generated functions:\n\n1. **Manual Conversions** in pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/conversion.go implement custom logic for:\n   - IngressBackend: Converts between \"ServiceName\"/\"ServicePort\" fields (v1beta1) and nested \"Service\" structure with ServiceBackendPort (internal/v1)\n   - IngressSpec: Handles \"Backend\" field (v1beta1) vs \"DefaultBackend\" field (internal/v1)\n\n2. **Extensions v1beta1 Conversions** in pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/conversion.go perform the same backend/service transformations for deprecated extensions/v1beta1 Ingress resources\n\n3. **Generated Conversions** in zz_generated.conversion.go files provide autogenerated conversion functions for all Ingress types between v1 and internal representations\n\n## PathType Validation Changes\nA critical difference between v1beta1 and v1 is PathType handling:\n\n- **v1beta1** (staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1beta1/types.go:307): PathType is optional with default \"omitempty\" annotation\n- **v1** (staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1/types.go:500): PathType is **required** (no omitempty)\n\nValidation in pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation.go:428-429 enforces that PathType must be specified during validation of HTTPIngressPath objects. This is the key breaking change in the v1beta1→v1 migration.\n\n## Defaulting Behavior\nBoth v1beta1 versions (networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 and extensions/v1beta1) provide defaulting for HTTPIngressPath.PathType:\n- pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/defaults.go:28-32 and pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/defaults.go:165-169 both set default PathType to PathTypeImplementationSpecific when PathType is nil\n- v1 (pkg/apis/networking/v1/defaults.go) does not provide Ingress-specific defaulting, ensuring PathType is explicitly required\n\n## Storage and REST Configuration\npkg/registry/networking/rest/storage_settings.go establishes the REST storage hierarchy:\n- v1Storage() (line 57) provides the primary storage for networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress resources\n- v1alpha1Storage() supports legacy v1beta1 API resources through compatibility layers\n- Both configure the same underlying storage backend using pkg/registry/networking/ingress/storage/storage.go\n\n## Ingress Validation Strategy\nThe pkg/registry/networking/ingress/strategy.go file implements the validation strategy that is applied during create/update operations:\n- Line 95-97: ValidateIngressCreate calls validation.ValidateIngressCreate\n- Line 121-122: ValidateUpdate calls validation.ValidateIngressUpdate\n- Lines 54-66: GetResetFields defines which fields can be reset per API version (extensions/v1beta1, networking.k8s.io/v1beta1, networking.k8s.io/v1)\n\n## Admission and Defaults\nPlugin pkg/admission/network/defaultingressclass/admission.go implements the DefaultIngressClass admission controller which applies defaults to Ingress objects that use the v1 API and manages the ingressClassName field.\n\n## API Registration\nThe version registration and scheme setup occurs in:\n- pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go: Sets up the scheme for all versions and establishes v1 as the preferred storage version\n- pkg/apis/extensions/install/install.go: Maintains backward compatibility with deprecated extensions/v1beta1\n- Individual v1/register.go and v1beta1/register.go files register version-specific conversions and defaults\n"
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Kubernetes Ingress v1beta1 to v1 Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement Ingress API handling for both v1beta1 and v1: the conversion functions between versions, the validation changes (pathType became required), and the storage version migration logic.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all the Go source files related to Ingress API v1beta1 to v1 migration in the kubernetes/kubernetes codebase. Let me start by discovering the available repositories and then systematically searching for the relevant files.
Let me search for Ingress API files in the kubernetes repository:
Now let me create a comprehensive answer file with all my findings:

Perfect! I've successfully compiled a comprehensive analysis of the Kubernetes Ingress API v1beta1 to v1 migration. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings:

**Storage Version:** v1 (networking.k8s.io/v1) is the primary storage version, with storage hash "39NQlfNR+bo="

**Major Changes in v1:**
1. **PathType becomes required** - In v1beta1 it's optional with a default, but in v1 it must be explicitly specified (validated at pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation.go:428-429)
2. **Backend field restructuring** - Changed from flat `ServiceName`/`ServicePort` fields to nested `Service` structure
3. **Version Priority** - Set via SetVersionPriority(v1, v1beta1, v1alpha1) in install.go

**Conversion Infrastructure:**
- Manual conversion functions in `pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/conversion.go` handle the backend/service transformations
- Parallel conversions in `pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/conversion.go` for deprecated extensions API
- Auto-generated conversions in `zz_generated.conversion.go` files
- Defaulting functions provide PathType=ImplementationSpecific fallback for v1beta1 versions

**Storage & API Configuration:**
- Unified REST storage in `storage_settings.go` with v1 as primary
- Ingress validation strategy enforces PathType requirement in v1
- Admission controllers apply additional defaults like IngressClass handling

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with comprehensive file paths, symbols, and a detailed narrative explaining the migration architecture.

  "text": "The Kubernetes Ingress API migration from v1beta1 to v1 (and from extensions/v1beta1) involves multiple layers of conversion, validation, and storage configuration. Here are the key findings:\n\n## Storage Version and Version Priority\nThe storage version is **v1** (networking.k8s.io/v1), as established in pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go:41 via SetVersionPriority(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1alpha1.SchemeGroupVersion). The storage version hash for ingresses/v1 is \"39NQlfNR+bo=\" (pkg/controlplane/storageversionhashdata/data.go:63).\n\n## Conversion Functions\nConversion between versions is handled through both manual and auto-generated functions:\n\n1. **Manual Conversions** in pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/conversion.go implement custom logic for:\n   - IngressBackend: Converts between \"ServiceName\"/\"ServicePort\" fields (v1beta1) and nested \"Service\" structure with ServiceBackendPort (internal/v1)\n   - IngressSpec: Handles \"Backend\" field (v1beta1) vs \"DefaultBackend\" field (internal/v1)\n\n2. **Extensions v1beta1 Conversions** in pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/conversion.go perform the same backend/service transformations for deprecated extensions/v1beta1 Ingress resources\n\n3. **Generated Conversions** in zz_generated.conversion.go files provide autogenerated conversion functions for all Ingress types between v1 and internal representations\n\n## PathType Validation Changes\nA critical difference between v1beta1 and v1 is PathType handling:\n\n- **v1beta1** (staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1beta1/types.go:307): PathType is optional with default \"omitempty\" annotation\n- **v1** (staging/src/k8s.io/api/networking/v1/types.go:500): PathType is **required** (no omitempty)\n\nValidation in pkg/apis/networking/validation/validation.go:428-429 enforces that PathType must be specified during validation of HTTPIngressPath objects. This is the key breaking change in the v1beta1→v1 migration.\n\n## Defaulting Behavior\nBoth v1beta1 versions (networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 and extensions/v1beta1) provide defaulting for HTTPIngressPath.PathType:\n- pkg/apis/networking/v1beta1/defaults.go:28-32 and pkg/apis/extensions/v1beta1/defaults.go:165-169 both set default PathType to PathTypeImplementationSpecific when PathType is nil\n- v1 (pkg/apis/networking/v1/defaults.go) does not provide Ingress-specific defaulting, ensuring PathType is explicitly required\n\n## Storage and REST Configuration\npkg/registry/networking/rest/storage_settings.go establishes the REST storage hierarchy:\n- v1Storage() (line 57) provides the primary storage for networking.k8s.io/v1 Ingress resources\n- v1alpha1Storage() supports legacy v1beta1 API resources through compatibility layers\n- Both configure the same underlying storage backend using pkg/registry/networking/ingress/storage/storage.go\n\n## Ingress Validation Strategy\nThe pkg/registry/networking/ingress/strategy.go file implements the validation strategy that is applied during create/update operations:\n- Line 95-97: ValidateIngressCreate calls validation.ValidateIngressCreate\n- Line 121-122: ValidateUpdate calls validation.ValidateIngressUpdate\n- Lines 54-66: GetResetFields defines which fields can be reset per API version (extensions/v1beta1, networking.k8s.io/v1beta1, networking.k8s.io/v1)\n\n## Admission and Defaults\nPlugin pkg/admission/network/defaultingressclass/admission.go implements the DefaultIngressClass admission controller which applies defaults to Ingress objects that use the v1 API and manages the ingressClassName field.\n\n## API Registration\nThe version registration and scheme setup occurs in:\n- pkg/apis/networking/install/install.go: Sets up the scheme for all versions and establishes v1 as the preferred storage version\n- pkg/apis/extensions/install/install.go: Maintains backward compatibility with deprecated extensions/v1beta1\n- Individual v1/register.go and v1beta1/register.go files register version-specific conversions and defaults\n"
}
